title: Why SoundHound AI (SOUN) Shares Are Getting Obliterated Today
text: SoundHound AI shares dropped 12.1% following the company's Q1 earnings announcement. While the voice AI firm exceeded revenue expectations with 51.7% year-over-year growth to $44.2 million, investors focused on disappointing profitability metrics. The primary concern centered on the company's losses. Management reported a GAAP loss of $0.11 per share, which was slightly worse than the $0.10 loss analysts had anticipated. Additionally, quarterly cash burn reached $26.73 million with an operating margin of -51.3%. The market reaction reflects a broader shift in investor priorities. The article notes that the negative investor reaction suggests a growing emphasis on profitability and positive cash flow for AI companies, not just rapid growth. This sell-off represents one of many volatile moves for the stock, which has declined 20.6% year-to-date and trades 60.7% below its October 2025 peak of $21.40.
